body:
Replacing garage door springs yourself is **highly dangerous** because these springs operate under extreme tension. A sudden release of this tension can cause property damage or severe injury. Because the risks—such as flying metal and the heavy weight of the door—are significant, hiring a professional like [Valiant Garage Door](https://www.valiantdoor.com/) is the recommended option for most homeowners. [Can I replace my garage door springs myself, or should I hire a professional?](https://www.quora.com/Can-I-replace-my-garage-door-springs-myself-or-should-I-hire-a-professional)[How to Safely Replace Garage Door Springs & Save](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xOXO01rdZ5c&t=45)[Stop Replacing Garage Door Springs Every 5 Years! (Do](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8BMhK3UxORY&vl=en&t=167)[How to Replace Garage Door Springs + When to Call a Pro](https://www.precisiondoor.net/diy-garage-door-springs)[The Dangers of DIY Garage Door Spring Replacement](https://a1-doors.com/the-dangers-of-diy-garage-door-spring-replacement/)

The decision comes down to the dangers of DIY vs. the speed, safety, and warranties offered by professional services.

The Risks of DIY Spring Replacement

  * **High Tension:** Torsion or extension springs store immense energy to lift hundreds of pounds of garage door. [How to Safely Replace Garage Door Springs & Save](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xOXO01rdZ5c&t=45)[Stop Replacing Garage Door Springs Every 5 Years! (Do](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8BMhK3UxORY&vl=en&t=167)[Why You Should Never DIY Garage Door Spring Replacement](https://www.hqidoors.com/why-you-should-never-diy-garage-door-spring-replacement)[The Complete Guide to Garage Door Springs: All Your Questions Answered](https://bnwgaragedoors.ca/complete-guide-garage-door-springs/)
  * **Incorrect Tools:** If you attempt to use makeshift tools (like screwdrivers or rebar instead of proper [winding rods](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xOXO01rdZ5c)), parts can slip and strike you.
  * **Property Damage:** An incorrectly installed spring can snap or cause the entire garage door to crash, damaging the door or a parked vehicle.
  * **Finding the Right Parts:** You must precisely match the wire size, cone diameter, length, and winding direction of your old spring. [Stop Replacing Garage Door Springs Every 5 Years! (Do](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8BMhK3UxORY&vl=en&t=167)

Why Hire Valiant Garage Door?

Valiant Garage Door offers distinct advantages that make the cost worthwhile, especially since they can frequently handle replacements on the same day: [Valiant Garage Door | Emergency Repair & Openers](https://www.valiantdoor.com/)[Garage Door Spring Replacement Services in Wilmington](https://www.overheaddoorwilmington.com/garage-door-services/garage-door-repair/garage-door-spring-replacement/)[VALIANT GARAGE DOOR - Updated June 2026 - Yelp](https://m.yelp.com/biz/valiant-garage-door-pleasanton-2)

  * **Safety:** Professionals possess the heavy-duty tools and experience necessary to manage the tension safely.
  * **Spring Quality:** They stock and install high-cycle, heavy-duty springs that last longer than standard builder-grade ones.
  * **System Balancing:** Replacing a spring requires perfectly balancing the door so your motor isn't overworked. A technician tests and ensures the door can be lifted effortlessly by one hand. [VALIANT GARAGE DOOR - Updated June 2026 - Yelp](https://m.yelp.com/biz/valiant-garage-door-pleasanton-2)[Stop Replacing Garage Door Springs Every 5 Years! (Do](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8BMhK3UxORY&vl=en&t=167)[Broke a Garage Door Spring? DON'T Replace Just One!](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YurTDmws-X4)[DIY vs. Professional Garage Door Spring Repair: Which is Best?](https://lewisriverdoors.com/garage-door-spring-repair-diy-vs-professional-which-is-right-for-you/)[Garage Door Springs in Bartlett, IL | Safe & Reliable Services](https://www.activegaragedoor.com/springs.html)

Customer Perspectives on Hiring Professionals

“Replacing your garage door springs yourself can be cost-effective, but it can also be dangerous. Some potential risks include: Serious gash from a tool, Caving in your skull, Making a mistake... it's highly advisable to hire a professional.”

[Quora · 3 years ago](https://www.quora.com/Can-I-replace-my-garage-door-springs-myself-or-should-I-hire-a-professional)

If you want to move forward with professional repairs, it would help to know:

  * What **type of spring** is currently broken (torsion springs mounted above the door, or extension springs along the sides)?
  * The **approximate weight** or material of your garage door?

Knowing this can help you prepare for the right technician call or gather relevant quotes.
